# CRIC-V — AI Cricket Coaching Platform

## What is CRIC-V?
CRIC-V (Cricket Vision) is a free, web-based AI cricket coaching platform built for grassroots cricket academies in Pakistan. It provides professional-grade biomechanical analysis using computer vision and machine learning — without requiring expensive hardware.

## Who built it?
Created by M. Talha Manzoor and Sameer Akram as a Final Year Project (FYP) for BS Information Technology at Lahore Garrison University (LGU), Pakistan, 2026. Supervised by Miss Rabia Younus.

## Core capabilities
- 33-point biomechanical pose estimation using MediaPipe
- 95%+ accuracy cricket ball tracking using YOLOv8
- ICC elbow compliance checking (15° limit enforcement)
- LSTM neural network shot classification (6 batting shot types)
- Pitch delivery heatmap generation
- Annotated video output with skeleton overlays
- AI-generated coaching drill recommendations
- YouTube video import support
- Multi-video session management
- Coach and player role-based access

## Technology stack
Frontend: Next.js, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
Backend: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, Celery, Redis
AI/ML: MediaPipe Pose, YOLOv8 (Ultralytics), PyTorch LSTM, OpenCV

## Target audience
Pakistani cricket coaches, cricket academy directors, players, sports technology researchers

## Pricing
Free to use. No hardware required beyond a standard HD camera or smartphone.

## Live URL
https://cric-v.live

## Academic context
This project addresses the gap between expensive professional cricket analysis systems (like Hawk-Eye, which costs millions) and traditional manual coaching methods used at grassroots level in Pakistan.

## Key differentiators
1. Works with any consumer camera — no specialized hardware
2. Free alternative to professional systems costing millions
3. Built specifically for Pakistani cricket context
4. Full pipeline from video upload to annotated output and drill recommendations
5. ICC bowling compliance enforcement
